event: Frontispiece for The Temptation of Saint Anthony
symbols: winged skull — the memento mori, death with wings suggesting the demonic temptations that assailed Saint Anthony
laurel or plant surrounding the skull — life and death intertwined, the victory wreath transformed into an emblem of mortality
description: A title page printed in orange ink on cream-colored paper. The text reads 'LA TENTATION DE SAINT-ANTOINE' in decorative lettering at the top, followed by 'Texte de GUSTAVE FLAUBERT' below. Between the title and the author credit, a small vignette illustration shows a winged skull or death's-head moth motif — a skull with insect-like wings and organic, plant-like tendrils. The design is printed in a single orange-brown color. Additional small text and what appears to be a signature are visible at the bottom of the page.

_meta.title: Frontpiece for The Temptation of Saint Anthony
